title: load_sapling_params reads and hashes 51.5 MB synchronously on every payment
description: load_sapling_params (tx_builder.rs) calls std::fs::read on both param files (47.9 MB + 3.6 MB) then BLAKE2b-512 hashes all 51.5 MB on every send_payment call. The std::fs::read is synchronous blocking I/O on the tokio async runtime. On cold page cache this is multi-second latency per payment. Fix: load and parse (SpendParameters, OutputParameters) once at chat() startup when wallet_fvks is Some; Arc-wrap and pass into the send_fn closure. Eliminates per-payment I/O entirely. Also: the blocking reads inside Box::pin(async move) should be tokio::task::spawn_blocking.
close_reason: Moved Sapling params loading to chat() startup; Arc-shared into send_fn closure; send_payment takes Option<&(SpendParameters, OutputParameters)>
